records.ety: [L. desuetudo, from desuescere, to grow out of use, disuse; de + suescere to become used or accustomed: cf. F. dsutude. See Custom.]

defs: The cessation of use; disuse; discontinuance of practice, custom, or fashion.
quotes: The desuetude abrogated the law, which, before, custom had established.
